mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and disposable income, driving equity valuations higher over the subsequent 20 trading days through elevated earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 140347k as of 2014-12-01 shows sustained employment growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth, the real interest rate turns negative, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and produces positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% and CPIAUCSL at 237.032 as of 2014-12-01
failure_condition: Negative Rank IC over any 2-year window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 58% of triggered instances despite negative real rate signal.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP growth above 2.5% annualized signals strengthening aggregate demand that expands corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as earnings revisions propagate.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16205.6B (2014-07-01 vintage) indicates post-crisis expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) in quarters following >2.5% growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.6% as of 2014-12-01 shows stable labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.03 (i.e., signal is not predictive of negative returns), or negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 52% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) decline month-over-month while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously contract, the joint signal indicates weakening consumer and construction demand that compresses forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline > 5% AND RSAFS month-over-month decline > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1028k and RSAFS at 442931M as of 2014-11-01 show demand strength
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.04 over any 2-year window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 48% of triggered instances despite joint demand weakness signal.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5% while remaining in a tightening monetary regime (FEDFUNDS > 0.5%), signaling supply-side weakness under policy constraint that reduces equity valuations over the subsequent 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.66 and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2014-11-01 show accommodative conditions
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with supply constraints
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.03 over any 3-year window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in periods matching trigger conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate from its current level signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses equity discount rates and increases asset valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days in post-crisis recovery regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2014-12-01 is near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines > 0.15pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously exceed 1000k units, signaling robust consumer and construction demand that drives positive earnings revisions and S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 1000k units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 442931M and HOUST at 1028k as of 2014-11-01 show strong demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window, or positive 5-day forward returns occur in < 62% of triggered instances despite joint demand strength signal.
